# Terraform Azure PostgreSQL Management Module

 [![Latest Release](https://img.shields.io/github/release/cloudopsworks/terraform-module-azurerm-postgres-management.svg?style=for-the-badge)](https://github.com/cloudopsworks/terraform-module-azurerm-postgres-management/releases/latest) [![Last Updated](https://img.shields.io/github/last-commit/cloudopsworks/terraform-module-azurerm-postgres-management.svg?style=for-the-badge)](https://github.com/cloudopsworks/terraform-module-azurerm-postgres-management/commits)


Terraform module for managing PostgreSQL databases, users, and roles on an existing Azure
PostgreSQL Flexible Server. Uses the `cyrilgdn/postgresql` provider for database-level
management and stores credentials securely in Azure Key Vault.


---

This project is part of our comprehensive approach towards DevOps Acceleration. 
[<img align="right" title="Share via Email" width="24" height="24" src="https://docs.cloudops.works/images/ionicons/ios-mail.svg"/>][share_email]

It's 100% Open Source and licensed under the [APACHE2](LICENSE).







We have [*lots of terraform modules*][terraform_modules] that are Open Source and we are trying to get them well-maintained!. Check them out!






## Introduction

This module manages PostgreSQL objects (databases, schemas, roles, and grants) on an existing
Azure PostgreSQL Flexible Server. It supports three connection modes:

- **Azure mode** (`azure.enabled=true`): Connects directly to an Azure PostgreSQL Flexible
  Server, optionally reading admin credentials from a Key Vault secret.
- **Hoop tunnel mode** (`hoop.enabled=true`): Routes connections through a local Hoop tunnel
  (localhost) for environments where the server is not directly reachable.
- **Direct mode** (default): Connects using explicit host/port/credentials.

All user credentials are stored as JSON secrets in Azure Key Vault. Enterprise Hoop mode
additionally stores per-field secrets (host, port, user, pass, db) and emits a structured
`hoop_connections` output consumed by the Hoop operator module.

## Usage


**IMPORTANT:** The `master` branch is used in `source` just as an example. In your code, do not pin to `master` because there may be breaking changes between releases.
Instead pin to the release tag (e.g. `?ref=vX.Y.Z`) of one of our [latest releases](https://github.com/cloudopsworks/terraform-module-azurerm-postgres-management/releases).


```hcl
# terragrunt.hcl
terraform {
  source = "git::https://github.com/cloudopsworks/terraform-module-azurerm-postgres-management.git//?ref=v1.0.0"
}

locals {
  local_vars = yamldecode(file("inputs.yaml"))
}

inputs = merge(
  local.local_vars,
  {
    org = {
      organization_name = "myorg"
      organization_unit = "platform"
      environment_name  = "production"
      environment_type  = "prod"
    }
    spoke_def  = "001"
    region     = "eastus2"
    extra_tags = {}
  }
)
```

**inputs.yaml** example:

```yaml
# Connection: Azure PostgreSQL Flexible Server (direct lookup)
azure:
  enabled: true               # (Optional) Use Azure Flexible Server lookup. Default: false.
  from_secret: false          # (Optional) Load credentials from Key Vault secret. Default: false.
  server_name: "mypg-server"  # (Required when enabled+!from_secret) Flexible Server name.
  resource_group_name: "rg-db" # (Optional) Server resource group.
  admin_username: "pgadmin"   # (Optional) Admin login. Default: "adminuser".
  admin_password: "S3cret!"   # (Optional) Admin password when not from_secret.
  db_name: "postgres"         # (Optional) Default database. Default: "postgres".
  sslmode: "require"          # (Optional) SSL mode. Default: "require".
  superuser: false            # (Optional) Superuser flag. Default: false.

# Connection: load admin creds from Key Vault JSON secret
# azure:
#   enabled: true
#   from_secret: true
#   secret_name: "mypg-admin-creds"  # (Required) KV secret with JSON {host,port,username,password,dbname}
#   server_name: "mypg-server"       # (Optional) Used as logical server name for hoop.

# Connection: Direct (no Azure lookup)
# direct:
#   host: "mypg-server.postgres.database.azure.com"  # (Required) FQDN or IP.
#   port: 5432                                        # (Optional) Port. Default: 5432.
#   username: "pgadmin"                               # (Required) Admin username.
#   password: "S3cret!"                               # (Required) Admin password.
#   db_name: "postgres"                               # (Optional) Default database. Default: "postgres".
#   server_name: "mypg-server"                        # (Optional) Logical name for credential naming.
#   sslmode: "require"                                # (Optional) SSL mode. Default: "require".
#   superuser: false                                  # (Optional) Superuser mode. Default: false.

# Databases
databases:
  mydb:                             # (Required) Logical key
    name: "mydb"                    # (Required) Actual database name
    owner: "appowner"               # (Optional) Owner role. Default: admin login.
    lc_collate: "en_US.UTF-8"       # (Optional) Collation. Default: "en_US.UTF-8".
    lc_ctype: "en_US.UTF-8"         # (Optional) Character type. Default: "en_US.UTF-8".
    connection_limit: -1            # (Optional) Max connections. Default: -1 (unlimited).
    schemas:                        # (Optional) Schemas to create in this database.
      - name: "app"
        owner: "appowner"

# Users / Roles
users:
  owner:                            # (Required) Logical key
    username: "appowner"            # (Required) PostgreSQL role name.
    databases: ["mydb"]             # (Required) Databases this user is associated with.
    create_owner: true              # (Optional) true=owner role, false=regular user. Default: true.
    create_db: false                # (Optional) Grant CREATEDB. Default: false.
    create_role: false              # (Optional) Grant CREATEROLE. Default: false.
  reader:
    username: "appreader"
    databases: ["mydb"]
    create_owner: false             # (Optional) Regular user. Default: true.
    access: "readonly"              # (Optional) "readonly" or "readwrite". Default: "readwrite".
    role: "readonly"                # (Optional) Grant membership in a named role group.

# Key Vault
key_vault_name: "my-keyvault"                   # (Required) Key Vault name for credential storage.
key_vault_resource_group_name: "rg-shared"      # (Required) Resource group of the Key Vault.

# Password management
password_rotation_period: "30d"   # (Optional) Rotation period. Default: "" (no rotation).
force_reset: false                 # (Optional) Force password reset on apply. Default: false.

# Hoop (optional)
hoop:
  enabled: false                   # (Optional) Enable Hoop integration. Default: false.
  community: true                  # (Optional) true=community (null output); false=enterprise. Default: true.
  agent_id: ""                     # (Required when enabled+!community) Hoop agent UUID.
  port: 5433                       # (Optional) Local Hoop tunnel port. Default: 5433.
  db_name: "postgres"              # (Optional) Database for Hoop connection. Default: "postgres".
  server_name: ""                  # (Optional) Logical server name for Hoop.
  import: false                    # (Optional) Import existing Hoop connection. Default: false.
  tags: {}                         # (Optional) Tags for Hoop connections.
  access_control: []               # (Optional) Access control groups.
```

## Quick Start

1. Ensure an Azure PostgreSQL Flexible Server exists and is reachable from your Terraform runner.
2. Ensure the Azure Key Vault exists and the runner's identity has `Secret Officer` permissions.
3. Copy `.boilerplate/inputs.yaml` to your Terragrunt deployment directory and fill in the required fields.
4. Run `terragrunt init && terragrunt plan` to preview changes.
5. Run `terragrunt apply` to create databases, roles, and Key Vault secrets.


## Examples

### Minimal — Azure Flexible Server with direct credentials

```yaml
azure:
  enabled: true
  server_name: "mypg-server"
  resource_group_name: "rg-db"
  admin_username: "pgadmin"
  admin_password: "S3cret!"

databases:
  appdb:
    name: "appdb"

users:
  appuser:
    username: "appuser"
    databases: ["appdb"]

key_vault_name: "mykv"
key_vault_resource_group_name: "rg-shared"
```

### With credentials from Key Vault secret

```yaml
azure:
  enabled: true
  from_secret: true
  secret_name: "mypg-admin-creds"

databases:
  appdb:
    name: "appdb"

users:
  appuser:
    username: "appuser"
    databases: ["appdb"]
    create_owner: false
    access: "readwrite"

key_vault_name: "mykv"
key_vault_resource_group_name: "rg-shared"
```

### Enterprise Hoop integration

```yaml
azure:
  enabled: true
  server_name: "mypg-server"
  resource_group_name: "rg-db"
  admin_username: "pgadmin"
  admin_password: "S3cret!"

databases:
  appdb:
    name: "appdb"

users:
  appowner:
    username: "appowner"
    databases: ["appdb"]

hoop:
  enabled: true
  community: false
  agent_id: "xxxxxxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxxxxxxxxxx"
  tags:
    team: "platform"

key_vault_name: "mykv"
key_vault_resource_group_name: "rg-shared"
```

## Inputs

| Name | Description | Type | Default | Required |
|------|-------------|------|---------|:--------:|
| <a name="input_azure"></a> [azure](#input\_azure) | Azure PostgreSQL Flexible Server connection settings. Mutually exclusive with direct/hoop. | `any` | `{}` | no |
| <a name="input_databases"></a> [databases](#input\_databases) | Map of PostgreSQL databases to create. | `any` | `{}` | no |
| <a name="input_direct"></a> [direct](#input\_direct) | Direct PostgreSQL connection settings. Used when azure.enabled=false and hoop.enabled=false. | `any` | `{}` | no |
| <a name="input_extra_tags"></a> [extra\_tags](#input\_extra\_tags) | Extra tags to add to the resources | `map(string)` | `{}` | no |
| <a name="input_force_reset"></a> [force\_reset](#input\_force\_reset) | (Optional) Force password reset for all managed users on next apply. Default: false. | `bool` | `false` | no |
| <a name="input_hoop"></a> [hoop](#input\_hoop) | Hoop connection configuration. Enterprise mode stores per-field secrets in Key Vault. | `any` | `{}` | no |
| <a name="input_is_hub"></a> [is\_hub](#input\_is\_hub) | Is this a hub or spoke configuration? | `bool` | `false` | no |
| <a name="input_key_vault_name"></a> [key\_vault\_name](#input\_key\_vault\_name) | (Required) Name of the existing Azure Key Vault for credential and hoop secret storage. | `string` | n/a | yes |
| <a name="input_key_vault_resource_group_name"></a> [key\_vault\_resource\_group\_name](#input\_key\_vault\_resource\_group\_name) | (Required) Resource group name of the existing Azure Key Vault. | `string` | n/a | yes |
| <a name="input_org"></a> [org](#input\_org) | Organization details | <pre>object({<br/>    organization_name = string<br/>    organization_unit = string<br/>    environment_type  = string<br/>    environment_name  = string<br/>  })</pre> | n/a | yes |
| <a name="input_password_rotation_period"></a> [password\_rotation\_period](#input\_password\_rotation\_period) | (Optional) Password rotation period for user credentials (e.g., '30d'). Default: empty (no rotation). | `string` | `""` | no |
| <a name="input_region"></a> [region](#input\_region) | Azure Region to deploy resources into. Example: 'eastus2', defaults to empty string as some of the resources may not require region setting. | `string` | `""` | no |
| <a name="input_spoke_def"></a> [spoke\_def](#input\_spoke\_def) | Spoke ID Number, must be a 3 digit number | `string` | `"001"` | no |
| <a name="input_users"></a> [users](#input\_users) | Map of PostgreSQL users/roles to create with their database assignments. | `any` | `{}` | no |

## Outputs

| Name | Description |
|------|-------------|
| <a name="output_databases"></a> [databases](#output\_databases) | Map of managed PostgreSQL databases. |
| <a name="output_hoop_connections"></a> [hoop\_connections](#output\_hoop\_connections) | Hoop connection definitions for Azure. Enterprise mode only (Key Vault has no sub-key access). Community mode returns null. |
| <a name="output_users"></a> [users](#output\_users) | Map of managed PostgreSQL users with their Key Vault credential secret names. |
